No downloads, installations or investment in technology infrastructure is required, and it's so easy to use, anyone can get up and running straight away.  
   
 ClassMarker is an online system, so you simply need internet access and a modern web browser. Additionally you will need JavaScript and cookies enabled which comes standard with all web browsers.  
   
 ClassMarker's online-testing system is available to anyone with an internet connection. Our web-based exam service is easy-to-use, secure and reliable.  
   
 ClassMarker was built from the ground up using valid XHTML and adhering to accessibility standards. This means ClassMarker works across all major operating systems, web browsers and devices to ensure our service can be accessed however and wherever you like. [...] When you assign a test you will be provided with a number of customizable settings to ensure your test is delivered according to your requirements. These include time & date availability, number of attempts allowed, whether a certificate is to be included, the pass mark, pass and fail feedback messages plus many more settings. You can also save Default Settings to streamline the assign process.  
   
 You can view test results in many ways with ClassMarker, from viewing an individual's progress in real time, to aggregated statistics for tests, questions, categories and more.  
   
 Don't forget to look for Export options - ClassMarker offers the ability to export detailed results reports to .CSV files to open in Spreadsheets where you can sort and filter the data as you require. [...] ClassMarker optionally allows you to integrate your own user ids with test results allowing you to easily export this data or send data (including test results) back to your own website in real time.  
   
 You can record your own users ids by automatically appending them to the Link your users click to take their test (if they are logged in to your website for example).  
   
 Example:  
   
   
 The cm\_user\_id will hold your users "user id for your system" and will save this against their test results.

Relying on a single course may limit understanding. Combine videos, articles, podcasts, and hands-on projects to reinforce concepts from different perspectives. Using multiple resources ensures a more complete grasp of the subject.

Gradescope has many features that helped to make the marking process as smooth as possible. Like any good online marking tool, it facilitates multiple markers in a couple of ways: the class can be split into sections with different markers for each, or different questions can be marked by different markers, or both. All markers can work on the same submission simultaneously and reuse comments left by other markers. Its rubric system allows marks awarded for particular points to be modified retrospectively across the whole class, simplifying moderation and standardisation. Marks are totalled automatically and are easily exported for upload to University systems. Marked scripts can be returned to students online as a matter of routine. New features are being added all the time; however, the [...] New features are being added all the time; however, the system is not perfect. For instance, support for marking using a tablet pen not as smooth as it could be, and returning a letter grade requires creative use of the rubrics since the design assumes marks are numerical.
